text.title: Global per-repo numbering
text.summary: Two separate global counters per repo. CRs and BUGs do NOT share namespace, and the counter is never reset.

rule.statement: The repo MAINTAINS exactly two global counters: one for CRs (`CR001`, `CR002`, …) and one for BUGs (`BUG001`, `BUG002`, …).
rule.rationale: Separate counters make release notes legible: "Sprint shipped 3 features, 5 CRs, 2 BUGs" is meaningful only when the streams do not share IDs.

rule.statement: Counters are global per repo (NOT per feature, NOT per release). Numbers are never reused.
rule.rationale: Reuse would break grep-based history. Past IDs in commit messages must still resolve.

section.title: Allocating the next number
section.p: The next CR or BUG ID is one higher than the maximum currently on disk in either `todo/` or `done/` (and `in-progress/` for BUGs).

example.code: # Next CR ls crs/{todo,done}/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'CR[0-9]+' | sort -V | tail -1 # Next BUG ls bugs/{todo,in-progress,done}/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'BUG[0-9]+' | sort -V | tail -1

antipattern.description: Resetting CR / BUG counters per quarter or per release.
antipattern.reason: Past commit messages reference `CR042` — if `CR042` now means something different, the history is broken.
